era_context: Han Dynasty under Emperor Xuan (r. 74–49 BCE). The Roman Republic is in the mid-2nd century BCE, consolidating control over the Eastern Mediterranean. The Parthian Empire is emerging as a major power in Mesopotamia and Persia, creating a buffer between Han China and the Roman world.

events.title: Han Dynasty military campaigns on the northern frontier
events.description: During Emperor Xuan's reign, Han China conducted periodic military campaigns against Xiongnu incursions and to secure control over frontier regions. These campaigns were part of a long-term strategy to maintain Han dominance over Central Asia and protect the Silk Road trade routes. The specific campaigns of 138 BCE are not well-documented in surviving sources, but this year falls within a period of active frontier military operations.
key_figures: Han military commanders (names not specifically attested for 138 BCE)

sources.name: Book of Han (Han Shu) by Ban Gu, compiled c. 111 CE

events.certainty_note: Military campaigns during Xuan's reign are documented in general terms, but specific events in 138 BCE are not precisely dated.

disconfirming_evidence: No specific military events are reliably dated to 138 BCE. The entry represents a generalization about Han frontier policy during this period.
historiographic_note: The historical record for 138 BCE is minimal. Surviving sources do not provide specific documentation for this year. Information is inferred from broader patterns of Han Dynasty military activity.
